---
name: Chore / maintenance
about: Refactor, perf, style, test, build, or CI work with no user-facing feature change
title: "🔧 chore: "
labels: chore
---

<!-- Umbrella template for non-feature, non-bug maintenance work. Use it for
internal-quality changes that don't add capability or fix broken behavior. -->

## Scope

<!-- Precisely which files, modules, or systems this touches. Keep it tight —
maintenance work that sprawls becomes risky. State what is in scope and, if
helpful, what is deliberately left alone. -->

## Rationale

<!-- Why do this now? E.g. unblocks a future feature, reduces flakiness, cuts
build time, pays down a specific debt. Tie it to a concrete benefit. -->

## Type of chore

<!-- Check all that apply. This maps to the Conventional-Commits type used for
the branch and commits. -->

- [ ] ♻️ refactor — restructure code without changing behavior
- [ ] ⚡️ perf — improve performance
- [ ] 🎨 style — formatting / whitespace, no logic change
- [ ] ✅ test — add or fix tests
- [ ] 🔧 build — build system, dependencies, packaging
- [ ] 🔧 ci — CI configuration and scripts

## Risk / Impact

<!-- What could break, and how will you know if it does? Note behavior that MUST
remain identical, the verification approach (tests pass before & after), and any
rollback considerations. -->
